skill.name: lark-openapi-explorer
skill.description: 飞书/Lark 原生 OpenAPI 探索：从官方文档库中挖掘未经 CLI 封装的原生 OpenAPI 接口。当用户的需求无法被现有 lark-* skill 或 lark-cli 已注册命令满足，需要查找并调用原生飞书 OpenAPI 时使用。
skill.summary: Discover raw Lark OpenAPI endpoints from official documentation when existing CLI commands are insufficient.
skill.author: larksuite

security_audit.summary: All 38 static analyzer findings are false positives. External command patterns are documented lark-cli usage examples (skill's intended purpose). Hardcoded URLs point to official Lark/Feishu OpenAPI documentation. Path traversal patterns are markdown relative links for cross-referencing documentation. The skill follows safe practices with mandatory user confirmation for write/delete operations.

risk_factor_evidence.context: Legitimate lark-cli command documentation examples - this is the skill's intended purpose
Official Lark/Feishu OpenAPI documentation URLs - safe public endpoints

low_findings.title: External Command Execution via lark-cli
low_findings.description: The skill executes shell commands through lark-cli for API calls. All commands are documented examples with explicit user intent required.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Pattern confirmed but usage is legitimate - lark-cli is a required dependency and commands require explicit user intent

low_findings.title: Network Access to External Documentation
low_findings.description: WebFetch is used to retrieve API documentation from open.feishu.cn and open.larksuite.com. These are official Lark documentation endpoints.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Network access confirmed to official documentation domains - low risk as these are public read-only endpoints

content.user_title: Discover raw Lark OpenAPI endpoints from documentation
content.value_statement: When existing CLI commands cannot meet your needs, this skill explores official Lark/Feishu documentation to find and invoke raw OpenAPI endpoints. It bridges the gap between pre-built commands and undocumented API capabilities.
actual_capabilities: Fetch and parse Lark/Feishu official OpenAPI documentation from open.feishu.cn or open.larksuite.com
Identify raw API endpoints not covered by existing lark-cli commands
Extract complete API specifications including HTTP method, URL path, parameters, and response format
Execute API calls through lark-cli api with proper authentication
Translate documentation from Chinese to English when requested
Provide safety warnings before destructive operations
limitations: Requires lark-cli to be installed and configured with valid credentials
Cannot access API endpoints not documented in official Lark/Feishu documentation
Write and delete operations require explicit user confirmation before execution
Rate limits and quotas are enforced by Lark API, not controlled by this skill

use_cases.title: Add members to a chat using undocumented API
use_cases.description: When the CLI lacks a built-in command to add chat members, this skill discovers the POST /open-apis/im/v1/chats/:chat_id/members endpoint and executes it with proper parameters.
use_cases.target_user: Team administrators managing Lark group memberships

use_cases.title: Set custom chat announcements
use_cases.description: Find and invoke the PATCH endpoint for chat announcements when no CLI shortcut exists, enabling custom group announcement management.

use_cases.title: Explore new API features before CLI support
use_cases.description: Access newly released Lark APIs immediately through documentation exploration, without waiting for CLI updates or new skill development.
use_cases.target_user: Developers building Lark integrations and automation workflows

prompt_templates.title: Find an API endpoint for a specific task
prompt_templates.prompt: I need to [describe task] in Lark. Search the official OpenAPI documentation to find the appropriate endpoint and show me how to call it with lark-cli api.

prompt_templates.title: Get complete API specification
prompt_templates.prompt: Look up the API documentation for [API name or endpoint]. Extract the HTTP method, URL path, required parameters, optional parameters, response format, and required permissions.

prompt_templates.title: Execute an API call with parameters
prompt_templates.prompt: Using the [endpoint name] API, call it with the following parameters: [list parameters]. Confirm the expected request and response before executing.

prompt_templates.title: Compare existing CLI commands with raw API
prompt_templates.prompt: Check if lark-cli has a built-in command for [task]. If not, explore the official documentation to find the raw OpenAPI endpoint and provide the equivalent lark-cli api command.

output_examples.input: Add users ou_123 and ou_456 to chat oc_789
What APIs are available for managing group announcements?
output: Parameters: revision (string), requests (array of HTML content)

best_practices: Always verify the API endpoint exists in official documentation before calling it
Review the required permissions (scope) and ensure your app has authorization
Use --dry-run or preview mode when available for write/delete operations
anti_patterns: Do not guess API paths or parameters - always retrieve from official documentation
Avoid calling write/delete APIs without confirming user intent and explaining impact
Do not skip the capability check step - existing CLI commands are preferred over raw API calls

faq.question: What is the difference between lark-cli commands and lark-cli api?
faq.answer: lark-cli commands are pre-built shortcuts for common tasks (like lark-cli im send). The lark-cli api command allows raw HTTP calls to any OpenAPI endpoint, useful when no shortcut exists.

faq.question: Do I need separate authentication to use this skill?
faq.answer: No. This skill uses the same lark-cli authentication configured for other Lark skills. Ensure lark-cli is properly set up with valid credentials before using.

faq.question: Can this skill access APIs not in the official documentation?
faq.answer: No. This skill only discovers and calls APIs documented at open.feishu.cn or open.larksuite.com. Undocumented or internal APIs cannot be accessed through this method.

faq.question: What happens if an API call fails?
faq.answer: The lark-cli will return the API error response including error code and message. Common issues include insufficient permissions, rate limiting, or invalid parameters.

faq.question: Can I use this skill with both Feishu and Lark accounts?
faq.answer: Yes. Specify the appropriate domain (open.feishu.cn for Feishu, open.larksuite.com for Lark) when exploring documentation. The skill defaults to Feishu if not specified.

faq.question: How do I know what permissions my API call needs?
faq.answer: Each API documentation page lists required scopes under the permissions section. Verify your Lark app has these scopes enabled in the developer console before calling.
